link|Q11421390}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kokuzō Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kokuzo jinjya.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}{{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya-machi|Q5986245|WD=Q5986245}}Teno 2100 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto
Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto
Takahashi-no-Kami
Hinomiya-no-Kami |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditional {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Kita-no-Miya |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|label=Kokuzō Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kokuzō Shrine''' (こくぞうじんじゃ / くにのみやつこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Teno, Ichinomiya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}. Because it stands north of former kanpei-taisha {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639}}, it is also called '''Kita-no-Miya'''. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194}} (ed.), ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p.375. === History and origin === The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists four shrines in {{ill|lt=Higo Province|Q259705|WD=Q259705}} (Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto Shrine = present {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639}}, Asohime Shrine = present Aso Shrine, Kokuzō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Hikino Shrine|Q17224057|WD=Q17224057}}). Kokuzō Shrine is one of these four and is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture. According to ''Higo no Kuni-shi'', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto was appointed Higo no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and in {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844}}), Kokuzō Shrine was repaired and rituals were organized''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture'', Heibonsha, 1985, p.310. With his father, Aso Shrine's principal deity {{ill|lt=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|Q10888904|WD=Q10888904}}, he is said to have developed the Aso area and taught agriculture and afforestation. The principal deity is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}''' (according to ''{{ill|lt=Kokuzō Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194}}'', the first '''{{ill|lt=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q56347021|WD=Q56347021}}'''), and together with his consort and sons, four deities are enshrinedEditorial Committee of Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary (ed.), ''Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.481. The current shrine buildings were built in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}}) by {{ill|lt=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi|Q11605971|WD=Q11605971}}, third lord of the {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Domain|Q1194248|WD=Q1194248|lt=Higo Kumamoto Domain}}Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture, Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.1297. It became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 5. About 60 m west of the torii are the '''Kamimikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of the enshrined deity Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto, and the '''Shimomikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of his consort Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto. These are late-6th-century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} with {{ill|lt=horizontal stone chambers|Q11543435|WD=Q11543435}}, built of huge {{ill|lt=andesite|Q181908|WD=Q181908}} blocks and cut Aso lava stone, and are representative megalithic kofun of Kumamoto. === Teno no Sugi === [[ファイル:Tenonosugi.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Preserved remains of Teno no Sugi]] A giant cedar known as "{{ill|lt=Teno no Sugi|Q17217752|WD=Q17217752}}" once stood in the precincts''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', p.116.''Natural Monuments of Japan'', p.151.. It was designated a national natural monument on December 9, 1924, under the then Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monuments Preservation LawMinistry of Home Affairs Notice No. 777, Taishō 13. It broke in Typhoon No. 19 (the "Apple Typhoon") on September 27, 1991, and in 2000 the Council for Cultural Affairs recommended de-designation[https://web.archive.org/web/20060111191246/http://www.bunka.go.jp/1hogo/main.asp%7B0fl=show&id=1000007342&clc=1000011213&cmc=1000007006&cli=1000007317&cmi=1000007325%7B9.html Historic Sites / Scenic Places / Natural Monuments (May 2000)] Agency for Cultural Affairs website, viewed 2013-06-10., and it was officially de-designated on September 6, 2000Ministry of Education Notice No. 150, 2000. {{Main|Teno no Sugi}} === Enshrined deities === * Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto - son of Aso Shrine principal deity Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto. Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōkami. Enshrined at the 11th shrine of Aso Shrine. * Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto - consort of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. Also called Kamachi-hime-no-Mikoto; sea deity goddess. Principal deity of {{ill|lt=Kōriura Shrine|Q11643225|WD=Q11643225}}. * Takahashi-no-Kami - second son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. * Hinomiya-no-Kami - third son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. === Annual festival day === * July 26 === Cultural properties === === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) === * Aso agricultural rites (designated jointly with Aso Shrine) ** Utaizome, spring festival, onda festival, wind festival, nemuri-nagashi, tanomi festival === Aso City designated tangible cultural property === * Shrine hall and haiden[http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/education/cultural-property/list_of_cultural-property/ Aso City website (list of designated cultural properties)] === Precinct shrines === * Kadomori-sha * Suijin-sha * Namazu-no-miya === Access === * 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Hōhi Main Line|Q870826|WD=Q870826}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji Station|Q6884211|WD=Q6884211}} * 45 km from {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Interchange|Q1655087|WD=Q1655087}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Suzuki Takashi (historian)|Q11648696|WD=Q11648696|lt=Suzuki Takashi}}, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194}}, 1980, pp.62-63 * New Kumamoto City History Editorial Committee, ''New Kumamoto City History, Supplementary Vol.2'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto|Q199889|WD=Q199889}}, 1996, pp.342-345 * Aso Koreyuki (ed.), ''Aso Shrine'', Gakuseisha, 2007, pp.218-220 * {{ill|lt=Numata Makoto|Q11554605|WD=Q11554605}} (ed.), ''Natural Monuments of Japan 5: Plants III'', {{ill|lt=Kodansha|Q726081|WD=Q726081}}, 1984. ISBN 4-06-180585-1, p.116. * Supervised by Cultural Properties Protection Department, Agency for Cultural Affairs, ''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Dai-ichi Hoki|UNKNOWN}}, 1981, p.151. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Oguni Ryōjinja|Q109299741|WD=Q109299741}} - enshrines Takahashi Ōkami and Hinomiya Ōkami. === External links === {{commonscat|Kokuzō-jinja|Kokuzō Shrine}} * [http://www.pref.kumamoto.jp/site/arinomama/kokuzoujinja.html Kokuzō Shrine (Kumamoto Prefecture site)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こくそうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Higo Province]] [[Category:熊本県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥後国の式内社]] [[Category:阿蘇神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の歴史]] [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11421390}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kokuzō Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kokuzo jinjya.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}{{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya-machi|Q5986245|WD=Q5986245}}Teno 2100 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto
Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto
Takahashi-no-Kami
Hinomiya-no-Kami |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditional {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Kita-no-Miya |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|label=Kokuzō Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kokuzō Shrine''' (こくぞうじんじゃ / くにのみやつこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Teno, Ichinomiya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}. Because it stands north of former kanpei-taisha {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639}}, it is also called '''Kita-no-Miya'''. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194}} (ed.), ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p.375. === History and origin === The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists four shrines in {{ill|lt=Higo Province|Q259705|WD=Q259705}} (Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto Shrine = present {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639}}, Asohime Shrine = present Aso Shrine, Kokuzō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Hikino Shrine|Q17224057|WD=Q17224057}}). Kokuzō Shrine is one of these four and is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture. According to ''Higo no Kuni-shi'', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto was appointed Higo no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and in {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844}}), Kokuzō Shrine was repaired and rituals were organized''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture'', Heibonsha, 1985, p.310. With his father, Aso Shrine's principal deity {{ill|lt=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|Q10888904|WD=Q10888904}}, he is said to have developed the Aso area and taught agriculture and afforestation. The principal deity is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (according to ''{{ill|lt=Kokuzō Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194}}'', the first '''{{ill|lt=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q56347021|WD=Q56347021}}'''), and together with his consort and sons, four deities are enshrinedEditorial Committee of Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary (ed.), ''Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.481. The current shrine buildings were built in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}}) by {{ill|lt=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi|Q11605971|WD=Q11605971}}, third lord of the {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Domain|Q1194248|WD=Q1194248|lt=Higo Kumamoto Domain}}Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture, Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.1297. It became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 5. About 60 m west of the torii are the '''Kamimikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of the enshrined deity Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto, and the '''Shimomikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of his consort Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto. These are late-6th-century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} with {{ill|lt=horizontal stone chambers|Q11543435|WD=Q11543435}}, built of huge {{ill|lt=andesite|Q181908|WD=Q181908}} blocks and cut Aso lava stone, and are representative megalithic kofun of Kumamoto. === Teno no Sugi === [[ファイル:Tenonosugi.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Preserved remains of Teno no Sugi]] A giant cedar known as "{{ill|lt=Teno no Sugi|Q17217752|WD=Q17217752}}" once stood in the precincts''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', p.116.''Natural Monuments of Japan'', p.151.. It was designated a national natural monument on December 9, 1924, under the then Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monuments Preservation LawMinistry of Home Affairs Notice No. 777, Taishō 13. It broke in Typhoon No. 19 (the "Apple Typhoon") on September 27, 1991, and in 2000 the Council for Cultural Affairs recommended de-designation[https://web.archive.org/web/20060111191246/http://www.bunka.go.jp/1hogo/main.asp%7B0fl=show&id=1000007342&clc=1000011213&cmc=1000007006&cli=1000007317&cmi=1000007325%7B9.html Historic Sites / Scenic Places / Natural Monuments (May 2000)] Agency for Cultural Affairs website, viewed 2013-06-10., and it was officially de-designated on September 6, 2000Ministry of Education Notice No. 150, 2000. {{Main|Teno no Sugi}} === Enshrined deities === * Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto - son of Aso Shrine principal deity Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto. Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōkami. Enshrined at the 11th shrine of Aso Shrine. * Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto - consort of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. Also called Kamachi-hime-no-Mikoto; sea deity goddess. Principal deity of {{ill|lt=Kōriura Shrine|Q11643225|WD=Q11643225}}. * Takahashi-no-Kami - second son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. * Hinomiya-no-Kami - third son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. === Annual festival day === * July 26 === Cultural properties === === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) === * Aso agricultural rites (designated jointly with Aso Shrine) ** Utaizome, spring festival, onda festival, wind festival, nemuri-nagashi, tanomi festival === Aso City designated tangible cultural property === * Shrine hall and haiden[http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/education/cultural-property/list_of_cultural-property/ Aso City website (list of designated cultural properties)] === Precinct shrines === * Kadomori-sha * Suijin-sha * Namazu-no-miya === Access === * 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Hōhi Main Line|Q870826|WD=Q870826}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji Station|Q6884211|WD=Q6884211}} * 45 km from {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Interchange|Q1655087|WD=Q1655087}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Suzuki Takashi (historian)|Q11648696|WD=Q11648696|lt=Suzuki Takashi}}, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194}}, 1980, pp.62-63 * New Kumamoto City History Editorial Committee, ''New Kumamoto City History, Supplementary Vol.2'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto|Q199889|WD=Q199889}}, 1996, pp.342-345 * Aso Koreyuki (ed.), ''Aso Shrine'', Gakuseisha, 2007, pp.218-220 * {{ill|lt=Numata Makoto|Q11554605|WD=Q11554605}} (ed.), ''Natural Monuments of Japan 5: Plants III'', {{ill|lt=Kodansha|Q726081|WD=Q726081}}, 1984. ISBN 4-06-180585-1, p.116. * Supervised by Cultural Properties Protection Department, Agency for Cultural Affairs, ''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Dai-ichi Hoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1981, p.151. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Oguni Ryōjinja|Q109299741|WD=Q109299741}} - enshrines Takahashi Ōkami and Hinomiya Ōkami. === External links === {{commonscat|Kokuzō-jinja|Kokuzō Shrine}} * [http://www.pref.kumamoto.jp/site/arinomama/kokuzoujinja.html Kokuzō Shrine (Kumamoto Prefecture site)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こくそうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Higo Province]] [[Category:熊本県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥後国の式内社]] [[Category:阿蘇神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の歴史]] [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditional {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Kita-no-Miya |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|label=Kokuzō Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kokuzō Shrine''' (こくぞうじんじゃ / くにのみやつこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Teno, Ichinomiya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}. Because it stands north of former kanpei-taisha {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639}}, it is also called '''Kita-no-Miya'''. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194}} (ed.), ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p.375. === History and origin === The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists four shrines in {{ill|lt=Higo Province|Q259705|WD=Q259705}} (Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto Shrine = present {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639}}, Asohime Shrine = present Aso Shrine, Kokuzō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Hikino Shrine|Q17224057|WD=Q17224057}}). Kokuzō Shrine is one of these four and is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture. According to ''Higo no Kuni-shi'', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto was appointed Higo no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and in {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844}}), Kokuzō Shrine was repaired and rituals were organized''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture'', Heibonsha, 1985, p.310. With his father, Aso Shrine's principal deity {{ill|lt=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|Q10888904|WD=Q10888904}}, he is said to have developed the Aso area and taught agriculture and afforestation. The principal deity is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (according to ''{{ill|lt=Kokuzō Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194}}'', the first '''{{ill|lt=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q56347021|WD=Q56347021}}'''), and together with his consort and sons, four deities are enshrinedEditorial Committee of Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary (ed.), ''Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.481. The current shrine buildings were built in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}}) by {{ill|lt=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi|Q11605971|WD=Q11605971}}, third lord of the {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Domain|Q1194248|WD=Q1194248|lt=Higo Kumamoto Domain}}Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture, Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.1297. It became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 5. About 60 m west of the torii are the '''Kamimikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of the enshrined deity Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto, and the '''Shimomikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of his consort Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto. These are late-6th-century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} with {{ill|lt=horizontal stone chambers|Q11543435|WD=Q11543435}}, built of huge {{ill|lt=andesite|Q181908|WD=Q181908}} blocks and cut Aso lava stone, and are representative megalithic kofun of Kumamoto. === Teno no Sugi === [[ファイル:Tenonosugi.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Preserved remains of Teno no Sugi]] A giant cedar known as "{{ill|lt=Teno no Sugi|Q17217752|WD=Q17217752}}" once stood in the precincts''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', p.116.''Natural Monuments of Japan'', p.151.. It was designated a national natural monument on December 9, 1924, under the then Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monuments Preservation LawMinistry of Home Affairs Notice No. 777, Taishō 13. It broke in Typhoon No. 19 (the "Apple Typhoon") on September 27, 1991, and in 2000 the Council for Cultural Affairs recommended de-designation[https://web.archive.org/web/20060111191246/http://www.bunka.go.jp/1hogo/main.asp%7B0fl=show&id=1000007342&clc=1000011213&cmc=1000007006&cli=1000007317&cmi=1000007325%7B9.html Historic Sites / Scenic Places / Natural Monuments (May 2000)] Agency for Cultural Affairs website, viewed 2013-06-10., and it was officially de-designated on September 6, 2000Ministry of Education Notice No. 150, 2000. {{Main|Teno no Sugi}} === Enshrined deities === * Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto - son of Aso Shrine principal deity Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto. Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōkami. Enshrined at the 11th shrine of Aso Shrine. * Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto - consort of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. Also called Kamachi-hime-no-Mikoto; sea deity goddess. Principal deity of {{ill|lt=Kōriura Shrine|Q11643225|WD=Q11643225}}. * Takahashi-no-Kami - second son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. * Hinomiya-no-Kami - third son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. === Annual festival day === * July 26 === Cultural properties === === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) === * Aso agricultural rites (designated jointly with Aso Shrine) ** Utaizome, spring festival, onda festival, wind festival, nemuri-nagashi, tanomi festival === Aso City designated tangible cultural property === * Shrine hall and haiden[http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/education/cultural-property/list_of_cultural-property/ Aso City website (list of designated cultural properties)] === Precinct shrines === * Kadomori-sha * Suijin-sha * Namazu-no-miya === Access === * 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Hōhi Main Line|Q870826|WD=Q870826}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji Station|Q6884211|WD=Q6884211}} * 45 km from {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Interchange|Q1655087|WD=Q1655087}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Suzuki Takashi (historian)|Q11648696|WD=Q11648696|lt=Suzuki Takashi}}, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194}}, 1980, pp.62-63 * New Kumamoto City History Editorial Committee, ''New Kumamoto City History, Supplementary Vol.2'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto|Q199889|WD=Q199889}}, 1996, pp.342-345 * Aso Koreyuki (ed.), ''Aso Shrine'', Gakuseisha, 2007, pp.218-220 * {{ill|lt=Numata Makoto|Q11554605|WD=Q11554605}} (ed.), ''Natural Monuments of Japan 5: Plants III'', {{ill|lt=Kodansha|Q726081|WD=Q726081}}, 1984. ISBN 4-06-180585-1, p.116. * Supervised by Cultural Properties Protection Department, Agency for Cultural Affairs, ''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Dai-ichi Hoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1981, p.151. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Oguni Ryōjinja|Q109299741|WD=Q109299741}} - enshrines Takahashi Ōkami and Hinomiya Ōkami. === External links === {{commonscat|Kokuzō-jinja|Kokuzō Shrine}} * [http://www.pref.kumamoto.jp/site/arinomama/kokuzoujinja.html Kokuzō Shrine (Kumamoto Prefecture site)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こくそうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Higo Province]] [[Category:熊本県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥後国の式内社]] [[Category:阿蘇神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の歴史]] [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditional {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Kita-no-Miya |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|label=Kokuzō Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kokuzō Shrine''' (こくぞうじんじゃ / くにのみやつこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Teno, Ichinomiya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}. Because it stands north of former kanpei-taisha {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639}}, it is also called '''Kita-no-Miya'''. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194}} (ed.), ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p.375. === History and origin === The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists four shrines in {{ill|lt=Higo Province|Q259705|WD=Q259705}} (Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto Shrine = present {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639}}, Asohime Shrine = present Aso Shrine, Kokuzō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Hikino Shrine|Q17224057|WD=Q17224057}}). Kokuzō Shrine is one of these four and is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture. According to ''Higo no Kuni-shi'', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto was appointed Higo no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and in {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844}}), Kokuzō Shrine was repaired and rituals were organized''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture'', Heibonsha, 1985, p.310. With his father, Aso Shrine's principal deity {{ill|lt=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|Q10888904|WD=Q10888904}}, he is said to have developed the Aso area and taught agriculture and afforestation. The principal deity is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (according to ''{{ill|lt=Kokuzō Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194}}'', the first '''{{ill|lt=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q56347021|WD=Q56347021}}'''), and together with his consort and sons, four deities are enshrinedEditorial Committee of Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary (ed.), ''Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.481. The current shrine buildings were built in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}}) by {{ill|lt=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi|Q11605971|WD=Q11605971}}, third lord of the {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Domain|Q1194248|WD=Q1194248|lt=Higo Kumamoto Domain}}Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture, Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.1297. It became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 5. About 60 m west of the torii are the '''Kamimikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of the enshrined deity Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto, and the '''Shimomikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of his consort Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto. These are late-6th-century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} with {{ill|lt=horizontal stone chambers|Q11543435|WD=Q11543435}}, built of huge {{ill|lt=andesite|Q181908|WD=Q181908}} blocks and cut Aso lava stone, and are representative megalithic kofun of Kumamoto. === Teno no Sugi === [[ファイル:Tenonosugi.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Preserved remains of Teno no Sugi]] A giant cedar known as "{{ill|lt=Teno no Sugi|Q17217752|WD=Q17217752}}" once stood in the precincts''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', p.116.''Natural Monuments of Japan'', p.151.. It was designated a national natural monument on December 9, 1924, under the then Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monuments Preservation LawMinistry of Home Affairs Notice No. 777, Taishō 13. It broke in Typhoon No. 19 (the "Apple Typhoon") on September 27, 1991, and in 2000 the Council for Cultural Affairs recommended de-designation[https://web.archive.org/web/20060111191246/http://www.bunka.go.jp/1hogo/main.asp%7B0fl=show&id=1000007342&clc=1000011213&cmc=1000007006&cli=1000007317&cmi=1000007325%7B9.html Historic Sites / Scenic Places / Natural Monuments (May 2000)] Agency for Cultural Affairs website, viewed 2013-06-10., and it was officially de-designated on September 6, 2000Ministry of Education Notice No. 150, 2000. {{Main|Teno no Sugi}} === Enshrined deities === * Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto - son of Aso Shrine principal deity Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto. Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōkami. Enshrined at the 11th shrine of Aso Shrine. * Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto - consort of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. Also called Kamachi-hime-no-Mikoto; sea deity goddess. Principal deity of {{ill|lt=Kōriura Shrine|Q11643225|WD=Q11643225}}. * Takahashi-no-Kami - second son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. * Hinomiya-no-Kami - third son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. === Annual festival day === * July 26 === Cultural properties === === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) === * Aso agricultural rites (designated jointly with Aso Shrine) ** Utaizome, spring festival, onda festival, wind festival, nemuri-nagashi, tanomi festival === Aso City designated tangible cultural property === * Shrine hall and haiden[http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/education/cultural-property/list_of_cultural-property/ Aso City website (list of designated cultural properties)] === Precinct shrines === * Kadomori-sha * Suijin-sha * Namazu-no-miya === Access === * 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Hōhi Main Line|Q870826|WD=Q870826}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji Station|Q6884211|WD=Q6884211}} * 45 km from {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Interchange|Q1655087|WD=Q1655087}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Suzuki Takashi (historian)|Q11648696|WD=Q11648696|lt=Suzuki Takashi}}, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194}}, 1980, pp.62-63 * New Kumamoto City History Editorial Committee, ''New Kumamoto City History, Supplementary Vol.2'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto|Q199889|WD=Q199889}}, 1996, pp.342-345 * Aso Koreyuki (ed.), ''Aso Shrine'', Gakuseisha, 2007, pp.218-220 * {{ill|lt=Numata Makoto|Q11554605|WD=Q11554605}} (ed.), ''Natural Monuments of Japan 5: Plants III'', {{ill|lt=Kodansha|Q726081|WD=Q726081}}, 1984. ISBN 4-06-180585-1, p.116. * Supervised by Cultural Properties Protection Department, Agency for Cultural Affairs, ''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Dai-ichi Hoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1981, p.151. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Oguni Ryōjinja|Q109299741|WD=Q109299741}} - enshrines Takahashi Ōkami and Hinomiya Ōkami. === External links === {{commonscat|Kokuzō-jinja|Kokuzō Shrine}} * [http://www.pref.kumamoto.jp/site/arinomama/kokuzoujinja.html Kokuzō Shrine (Kumamoto Prefecture site)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こくそうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Higo Province]] [[Category:熊本県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥後国の式内社]] [[Category:阿蘇神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の歴史]] [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}{{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya-machi|Q5986245|WD=Q5986245}}Teno 2100 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto
Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto
Takahashi-no-Kami
Hinomiya-no-Kami |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditional {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |別名 = Kita-no-Miya |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|label=Kokuzō Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kokuzō Shrine''' (こくぞうじんじゃ / くにのみやつこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Teno, Ichinomiya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308}}. Because it stands north of former kanpei-taisha {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639}}, it is also called '''Kita-no-Miya'''. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194}} (ed.), ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p.375. === History and origin === The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} lists four shrines in {{ill|lt=Higo Province|Q259705|WD=Q259705}} (Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto Shrine = present {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639}}, Asohime Shrine = present Aso Shrine, Kokuzō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Hikino Shrine|Q17224057|WD=Q17224057}}). Kokuzō Shrine is one of these four and is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture. According to ''Higo no Kuni-shi'', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto was appointed Higo no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and in {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844}}), Kokuzō Shrine was repaired and rituals were organized''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture'', Heibonsha, 1985, p.310. With his father, Aso Shrine's principal deity {{ill|lt=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|Q10888904|WD=Q10888904}}, he is said to have developed the Aso area and taught agriculture and afforestation. The principal deity is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (according to ''{{ill|lt=Kokuzō Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194}}'', the first '''{{ill|lt=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q56347021|WD=Q56347021}}'''), and together with his consort and sons, four deities are enshrinedEditorial Committee of Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary (ed.), ''Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.481. The current shrine buildings were built in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614}}) by {{ill|lt=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi|Q11605971|WD=Q11605971}}, third lord of the {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Domain|Q1194248|WD=Q1194248|lt=Higo Kumamoto Domain}}Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture, Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.1297. It became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 5. About 60 m west of the torii are the '''Kamimikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of the enshrined deity Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto, and the '''Shimomikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of his consort Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto. These are late-6th-century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747}} with {{ill|lt=horizontal stone chambers|Q11543435|WD=Q11543435}}, built of huge {{ill|lt=andesite|Q181908|WD=Q181908}} blocks and cut Aso lava stone, and are representative megalithic kofun of Kumamoto. === Teno no Sugi === [[ファイル:Tenonosugi.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Preserved remains of Teno no Sugi]] A giant cedar known as "{{ill|lt=Teno no Sugi|Q17217752|WD=Q17217752}}" once stood in the precincts''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', p.116.''Natural Monuments of Japan'', p.151.. It was designated a national natural monument on December 9, 1924, under the then Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monuments Preservation LawMinistry of Home Affairs Notice No. 777, Taishō 13. It broke in Typhoon No. 19 (the "Apple Typhoon") on September 27, 1991, and in 2000 the Council for Cultural Affairs recommended de-designation[https://web.archive.org/web/20060111191246/http://www.bunka.go.jp/1hogo/main.asp%7B0fl=show&id=1000007342&clc=1000011213&cmc=1000007006&cli=1000007317&cmi=1000007325%7B9.html Historic Sites / Scenic Places / Natural Monuments (May 2000)] Agency for Cultural Affairs website, viewed 2013-06-10., and it was officially de-designated on September 6, 2000Ministry of Education Notice No. 150, 2000. {{Main|Teno no Sugi}} === Enshrined deities === * Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto - son of Aso Shrine principal deity Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto. Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōkami. Enshrined at the 11th shrine of Aso Shrine. * Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto - consort of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. Also called Kamachi-hime-no-Mikoto; sea deity goddess. Principal deity of {{ill|lt=Kōriura Shrine|Q11643225|WD=Q11643225}}. * Takahashi-no-Kami - second son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. * Hinomiya-no-Kami - third son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. === Annual festival day === * July 26 === Cultural properties === === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) === * Aso agricultural rites (designated jointly with Aso Shrine) ** Utaizome, spring festival, onda festival, wind festival, nemuri-nagashi, tanomi festival === Aso City designated tangible cultural property === * Shrine hall and haiden[http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/education/cultural-property/list_of_cultural-property/ Aso City website (list of designated cultural properties)] === Precinct shrines === * Kadomori-sha * Suijin-sha * Namazu-no-miya === Access === * 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Hōhi Main Line|Q870826|WD=Q870826}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji Station|Q6884211|WD=Q6884211}} * 45 km from {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Interchange|Q1655087|WD=Q1655087}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Suzuki Takashi (historian)|Q11648696|WD=Q11648696|lt=Suzuki Takashi}}, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194}}, 1980, pp.62-63 * New Kumamoto City History Editorial Committee, ''New Kumamoto City History, Supplementary Vol.2'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto|Q199889|WD=Q199889}}, 1996, pp.342-345 * Aso Koreyuki (ed.), ''Aso Shrine'', Gakuseisha, 2007, pp.218-220 * {{ill|lt=Numata Makoto|Q11554605|WD=Q11554605}} (ed.), ''Natural Monuments of Japan 5: Plants III'', {{ill|lt=Kodansha|Q726081|WD=Q726081}}, 1984. ISBN 4-06-180585-1, p.116. * Supervised by Cultural Properties Protection Department, Agency for Cultural Affairs, ''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Dai-ichi Hoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1981, p.151. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Oguni Ryōjinja|Q109299741|WD=Q109299741}} - enshrines Takahashi Ōkami and Hinomiya Ōkami. === External links === {{commonscat|Kokuzō-jinja|Kokuzō Shrine}} * [http://www.pref.kumamoto.jp/site/arinomama/kokuzoujinja.html Kokuzō Shrine (Kumamoto Prefecture site)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こくそうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Higo Province]] [[Category:熊本県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥後国の式内社]] [[Category:阿蘇神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の歴史]] [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11421390}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kokuzō Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kokuzo jinjya.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681|1=Aso, Kumamoto}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya-machi|Q5986245|WD=Q5986245|1=Ichinomiya, Kumamoto}}Teno 2100 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto
Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto
Takahashi-no-Kami
Hinomiya-no-Kami |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditional {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844|1=AD 88}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kita-no-Miya |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|label=Kokuzō Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kokuzō Shrine''' (こくぞうじんじゃ / くにのみやつこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Teno, Ichinomiya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681|1=Aso, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. Because it stands north of former kanpei-taisha {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639|1=Aso Shrine}}, it is also called '''Kita-no-Miya'''. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun}} (ed.), ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p.375. === History and origin === The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} lists four shrines in {{ill|lt=Higo Province|Q259705|WD=Q259705|1=Higo Province}} (Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto Shrine = present {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639|1=Aso Shrine}}, Asohime Shrine = present Aso Shrine, Kokuzō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Hikino Shrine|Q17224057|WD=Q17224057|1=Hikino Shrine}}). Kokuzō Shrine is one of these four and is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture. According to ''Higo no Kuni-shi'', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto was appointed Higo no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and in {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844|1=AD 88}}), Kokuzō Shrine was repaired and rituals were organized''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture'', Heibonsha, 1985, p.310. With his father, Aso Shrine's principal deity {{ill|lt=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|Q10888904|WD=Q10888904|1=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto}}, he is said to have developed the Aso area and taught agriculture and afforestation. The principal deity is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (according to ''{{ill|lt=Kokuzō Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'', the first '''{{ill|lt=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q56347021|WD=Q56347021|1=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko}}'''), and together with his consort and sons, four deities are enshrinedEditorial Committee of Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary (ed.), ''Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.481. The current shrine buildings were built in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614|1=1672}}) by {{ill|lt=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi|Q11605971|WD=Q11605971|1=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi}}, third lord of the {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Domain|Q1194248|WD=Q1194248|lt=Higo Kumamoto Domain|1=Kumamoto Domain}}Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture, Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.1297. It became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 5. About 60 m west of the torii are the '''Kamimikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of the enshrined deity Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto, and the '''Shimomikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of his consort Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto. These are late-6th-century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} with {{ill|lt=horizontal stone chambers|Q11543435|WD=Q11543435|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, built of huge {{ill|lt=andesite|Q181908|WD=Q181908|1=Andesite}} blocks and cut Aso lava stone, and are representative megalithic kofun of Kumamoto. === Teno no Sugi === [[ファイル:Tenonosugi.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Preserved remains of Teno no Sugi]] A giant cedar known as "{{ill|lt=Teno no Sugi|Q17217752|WD=Q17217752|1=Teno No Sugi}}" once stood in the precincts''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', p.116.''Natural Monuments of Japan'', p.151.. It was designated a national natural monument on December 9, 1924, under the then Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monuments Preservation LawMinistry of Home Affairs Notice No. 777, Taishō 13. It broke in Typhoon No. 19 (the "Apple Typhoon") on September 27, 1991, and in 2000 the Council for Cultural Affairs recommended de-designation[https://web.archive.org/web/20060111191246/http://www.bunka.go.jp/1hogo/main.asp%7B0fl=show&id=1000007342&clc=1000011213&cmc=1000007006&cli=1000007317&cmi=1000007325%7B9.html Historic Sites / Scenic Places / Natural Monuments (May 2000)] Agency for Cultural Affairs website, viewed 2013-06-10., and it was officially de-designated on September 6, 2000Ministry of Education Notice No. 150, 2000. {{Main|Teno no Sugi}} === Enshrined deities === * Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto - son of Aso Shrine principal deity Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto. Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōkami. Enshrined at the 11th shrine of Aso Shrine. * Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto - consort of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. Also called Kamachi-hime-no-Mikoto; sea deity goddess. Principal deity of {{ill|lt=Kōriura Shrine|Q11643225|WD=Q11643225|1=Konoura Shrine}}. * Takahashi-no-Kami - second son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. * Hinomiya-no-Kami - third son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. === Annual festival day === * July 26 === Cultural properties === === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) === * Aso agricultural rites (designated jointly with Aso Shrine) ** Utaizome, spring festival, onda festival, wind festival, nemuri-nagashi, tanomi festival === Aso City designated tangible cultural property === * Shrine hall and haiden[http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/education/cultural-property/list_of_cultural-property/ Aso City website (list of designated cultural properties)] === Precinct shrines === * Kadomori-sha * Suijin-sha * Namazu-no-miya === Access === * 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Hōhi Main Line|Q870826|WD=Q870826|1=Hōhi Main Line}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji Station|Q6884211|WD=Q6884211|1=Miyaji Station}} * 45 km from {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Interchange|Q1655087|WD=Q1655087|1=Kumamoto interchange}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Suzuki Takashi (historian)|Q11648696|WD=Q11648696|lt=Suzuki Takashi|1=Takashi Suzuki}}, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun}}, 1980, pp.62-63 * New Kumamoto City History Editorial Committee, ''New Kumamoto City History, Supplementary Vol.2'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto|Q199889|WD=Q199889|1=Kumamoto}}, 1996, pp.342-345 * Aso Koreyuki (ed.), ''Aso Shrine'', Gakuseisha, 2007, pp.218-220 * {{ill|lt=Numata Makoto|Q11554605|WD=Q11554605|1=Makoto Numata}} (ed.), ''Natural Monuments of Japan 5: Plants III'', {{ill|lt=Kodansha|Q726081|WD=Q726081|1=Kodansha}}, 1984. ISBN 4-06-180585-1, p.116. * Supervised by Cultural Properties Protection Department, Agency for Cultural Affairs, ''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Dai-ichi Hoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1981, p.151. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Oguni Ryōjinja|Q109299741|WD=Q109299741|1=Oguni Ryo Shrine}} - enshrines Takahashi Ōkami and Hinomiya Ōkami. === External links === {{commonscat|Kokuzō-jinja|Kokuzō Shrine}} * [http://www.pref.kumamoto.jp/site/arinomama/kokuzoujinja.html Kokuzō Shrine (Kumamoto Prefecture site)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こくそうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Higo Province]] [[Category:熊本県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥後国の式内社]] [[Category:阿蘇神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の歴史]] [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11421390}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kokuzō Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kokuzo jinjya.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681|1=Aso, Kumamoto}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya-machi|Q5986245|WD=Q5986245|1=Ichinomiya, Kumamoto}}Teno 2100 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto
Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto
Takahashi-no-Kami
Hinomiya-no-Kami |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditional {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844|1=AD 88}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kita-no-Miya |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|label=Kokuzō Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kokuzō Shrine''' (こくぞうじんじゃ / くにのみやつこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Teno, Ichinomiya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681|1=Aso, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. Because it stands north of former kanpei-taisha {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639|1=Aso Shrine}}, it is also called '''Kita-no-Miya'''. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun}} (ed.), ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p.375. === History and origin === The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} lists four shrines in {{ill|lt=Higo Province|Q259705|WD=Q259705|1=Higo Province}} (Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto Shrine = present {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639|1=Aso Shrine}}, Asohime Shrine = present Aso Shrine, Kokuzō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Hikino Shrine|Q17224057|WD=Q17224057|1=Hikino Shrine}}). Kokuzō Shrine is one of these four and is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture. According to ''Higo no Kuni-shi'', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto was appointed Higo no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and in {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844|1=AD 88}}), Kokuzō Shrine was repaired and rituals were organized''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture'', Heibonsha, 1985, p.310. With his father, Aso Shrine's principal deity {{ill|lt=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|Q10888904|WD=Q10888904|1=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto}}, he is said to have developed the Aso area and taught agriculture and afforestation. The principal deity is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (according to ''{{ill|lt=Kokuzō Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'', the first '''{{ill|lt=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q56347021|WD=Q56347021|1=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko}}'''), and together with his consort and sons, four deities are enshrinedEditorial Committee of Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary (ed.), ''Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.481. The current shrine buildings were built in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614|1=1672}}) by {{ill|lt=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi|Q11605971|WD=Q11605971|1=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi}}, third lord of the {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Domain|Q1194248|WD=Q1194248|lt=Higo Kumamoto Domain|1=Kumamoto Domain}}Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture, Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.1297. It became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 5. About 60 m west of the torii are the '''Kamimikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of the enshrined deity Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto, and the '''Shimomikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of his consort Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto. These are late-6th-century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} with {{ill|lt=horizontal stone chambers|Q11543435|WD=Q11543435|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, built of huge {{ill|lt=andesite|Q181908|WD=Q181908|1=Andesite}} blocks and cut Aso lava stone, and are representative megalithic kofun of Kumamoto. === Teno no Sugi === [[ファイル:Tenonosugi.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Preserved remains of Teno no Sugi]] A giant cedar known as "{{ill|lt=Teno no Sugi|Q17217752|WD=Q17217752|1=Teno No Sugi}}" once stood in the precincts''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', p.116.''Natural Monuments of Japan'', p.151.. It was designated a national natural monument on December 9, 1924, under the then Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monuments Preservation LawMinistry of Home Affairs Notice No. 777, Taishō 13. It broke in Typhoon No. 19 (the "Apple Typhoon") on September 27, 1991, and in 2000 the Council for Cultural Affairs recommended de-designation[https://web.archive.org/web/20060111191246/http://www.bunka.go.jp/1hogo/main.asp%7B0fl=show&id=1000007342&clc=1000011213&cmc=1000007006&cli=1000007317&cmi=1000007325%7B9.html Historic Sites / Scenic Places / Natural Monuments (May 2000)] Agency for Cultural Affairs website, viewed 2013-06-10., and it was officially de-designated on September 6, 2000Ministry of Education Notice No. 150, 2000. {{Main|Teno no Sugi}} === Enshrined deities === * Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto - son of Aso Shrine principal deity Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto. Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōkami. Enshrined at the 11th shrine of Aso Shrine. * Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto - consort of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. Also called Kamachi-hime-no-Mikoto; sea deity goddess. Principal deity of {{ill|lt=Kōriura Shrine|Q11643225|WD=Q11643225|1=Konoura Shrine}}. * Takahashi-no-Kami - second son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. * Hinomiya-no-Kami - third son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. === Annual festival day === * July 26 === Cultural properties === === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) === * Aso agricultural rites (designated jointly with Aso Shrine) ** Utaizome, spring festival, onda festival, wind festival, nemuri-nagashi, tanomi festival === Aso City designated tangible cultural property === * Shrine hall and haiden[http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/education/cultural-property/list_of_cultural-property/ Aso City website (list of designated cultural properties)] === Precinct shrines === * Kadomori-sha * Suijin-sha * Namazu-no-miya === Access === * 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Hōhi Main Line|Q870826|WD=Q870826|1=Hōhi Main Line}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji Station|Q6884211|WD=Q6884211|1=Miyaji Station}} * 45 km from {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Interchange|Q1655087|WD=Q1655087|1=Kumamoto interchange}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Suzuki Takashi (historian)|Q11648696|WD=Q11648696|lt=Suzuki Takashi|1=Takashi Suzuki}}, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun}}, 1980, pp.62-63 * New Kumamoto City History Editorial Committee, ''New Kumamoto City History, Supplementary Vol.2'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto|Q199889|WD=Q199889|1=Kumamoto}}, 1996, pp.342-345 * Aso Koreyuki (ed.), ''Aso Shrine'', Gakuseisha, 2007, pp.218-220 * {{ill|lt=Numata Makoto|Q11554605|WD=Q11554605|1=Makoto Numata}} (ed.), ''Natural Monuments of Japan 5: Plants III'', {{ill|lt=Kodansha|Q726081|WD=Q726081|1=Kodansha}}, 1984. ISBN 4-06-180585-1, p.116. * Supervised by Cultural Properties Protection Department, Agency for Cultural Affairs, ''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Dai-ichi Hoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1981, p.151. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Oguni Ryōjinja|Q109299741|WD=Q109299741|1=Oguni Ryo Shrine}} - enshrines Takahashi Ōkami and Hinomiya Ōkami. === External links === {{commonscat|Kokuzō-jinja|Kokuzō Shrine}} * [http://www.pref.kumamoto.jp/site/arinomama/kokuzoujinja.html Kokuzō Shrine (Kumamoto Prefecture site)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こくそうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Higo Province]] [[Category:熊本県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥後国の式内社]] [[Category:阿蘇神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の歴史]] [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11421390}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kokuzō Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kokuzo jinjya.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681|1=Aso, Kumamoto}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya-machi|Q5986245|WD=Q5986245|1=Ichinomiya, Kumamoto}}Teno 2100 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto
Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto
Takahashi-no-Kami
Hinomiya-no-Kami |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditional {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844|1=AD 88}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kita-no-Miya |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|label=Kokuzō Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kokuzō Shrine''' (こくぞうじんじゃ / くにのみやつこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Teno, Ichinomiya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681|1=Aso, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. Because it stands north of former kanpei-taisha {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639|1=Aso Shrine}}, it is also called '''Kita-no-Miya'''. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun}} (ed.), ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p.375. === History and origin === The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} lists four shrines in {{ill|lt=Higo Province|Q259705|WD=Q259705|1=Higo Province}} (Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto Shrine = present {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639|1=Aso Shrine}}, Asohime Shrine = present Aso Shrine, Kokuzō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Hikino Shrine|Q17224057|WD=Q17224057|1=Hikino Shrine}}). Kokuzō Shrine is one of these four and is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture. According to ''Higo no Kuni-shi'', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto was appointed Higo no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and in {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844|1=AD 88}}), Kokuzō Shrine was repaired and rituals were organized''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture'', Heibonsha, 1985, p.310. With his father, Aso Shrine's principal deity {{ill|lt=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|Q10888904|WD=Q10888904|1=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto}}, he is said to have developed the Aso area and taught agriculture and afforestation. The principal deity is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (according to ''{{ill|lt=Kokuzō Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'', the first '''{{ill|lt=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q56347021|WD=Q56347021|1=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko}}'''), and together with his consort and sons, four deities are enshrinedEditorial Committee of Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary (ed.), ''Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.481. The current shrine buildings were built in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614|1=1672}}) by {{ill|lt=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi|Q11605971|WD=Q11605971|1=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi}}, third lord of the {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Domain|Q1194248|WD=Q1194248|lt=Higo Kumamoto Domain|1=Kumamoto Domain}}Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture, Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.1297. It became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 5. About 60 m west of the torii are the '''Kamimikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of the enshrined deity Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto, and the '''Shimomikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of his consort Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto. These are late-6th-century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} with {{ill|lt=horizontal stone chambers|Q11543435|WD=Q11543435|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, built of huge {{ill|lt=andesite|Q181908|WD=Q181908|1=Andesite}} blocks and cut Aso lava stone, and are representative megalithic kofun of Kumamoto. === Teno no Sugi === [[ファイル:Tenonosugi.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Preserved remains of Teno no Sugi]] A giant cedar known as "{{ill|lt=Teno no Sugi|Q17217752|WD=Q17217752|1=Teno No Sugi}}" once stood in the precincts''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', p.116.''Natural Monuments of Japan'', p.151.. It was designated a national natural monument on December 9, 1924, under the then Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monuments Preservation LawMinistry of Home Affairs Notice No. 777, Taishō 13. It broke in Typhoon No. 19 (the "Apple Typhoon") on September 27, 1991, and in 2000 the Council for Cultural Affairs recommended de-designation[https://web.archive.org/web/20060111191246/http://www.bunka.go.jp/1hogo/main.asp%7B0fl=show&id=1000007342&clc=1000011213&cmc=1000007006&cli=1000007317&cmi=1000007325%7B9.html Historic Sites / Scenic Places / Natural Monuments (May 2000)] Agency for Cultural Affairs website, viewed 2013-06-10., and it was officially de-designated on September 6, 2000Ministry of Education Notice No. 150, 2000. {{Main|Teno no Sugi}} === Enshrined deities === * Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto - son of Aso Shrine principal deity Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto. Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōkami. Enshrined at the 11th shrine of Aso Shrine. * Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto - consort of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. Also called Kamachi-hime-no-Mikoto; sea deity goddess. Principal deity of {{ill|lt=Kōriura Shrine|Q11643225|WD=Q11643225|1=Konoura Shrine}}. * Takahashi-no-Kami - second son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. * Hinomiya-no-Kami - third son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. === Annual festival day === * July 26 === Cultural properties === === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) === * Aso agricultural rites (designated jointly with Aso Shrine) ** Utaizome, spring festival, onda festival, wind festival, nemuri-nagashi, tanomi festival === Aso City designated tangible cultural property === * Shrine hall and haiden[http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/education/cultural-property/list_of_cultural-property/ Aso City website (list of designated cultural properties)] === Precinct shrines === * Kadomori-sha * Suijin-sha * Namazu-no-miya === Access === * 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Hōhi Main Line|Q870826|WD=Q870826|1=Hōhi Main Line}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji Station|Q6884211|WD=Q6884211|1=Miyaji Station}} * 45 km from {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Interchange|Q1655087|WD=Q1655087|1=Kumamoto interchange}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Suzuki Takashi (historian)|Q11648696|WD=Q11648696|lt=Suzuki Takashi|1=Takashi Suzuki}}, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun}}, 1980, pp.62-63 * New Kumamoto City History Editorial Committee, ''New Kumamoto City History, Supplementary Vol.2'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto|Q199889|WD=Q199889|1=Kumamoto}}, 1996, pp.342-345 * Aso Koreyuki (ed.), ''Aso Shrine'', Gakuseisha, 2007, pp.218-220 * {{ill|lt=Numata Makoto|Q11554605|WD=Q11554605|1=Makoto Numata}} (ed.), ''Natural Monuments of Japan 5: Plants III'', {{ill|lt=Kodansha|Q726081|WD=Q726081|1=Kodansha}}, 1984. ISBN 4-06-180585-1, p.116. * Supervised by Cultural Properties Protection Department, Agency for Cultural Affairs, ''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Dai-ichi Hoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1981, p.151. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Oguni Ryōjinja|Q109299741|WD=Q109299741|1=Oguni Ryo Shrine}} - enshrines Takahashi Ōkami and Hinomiya Ōkami. === External links === {{commonscat|Kokuzō-jinja|Kokuzō Shrine}} * [http://www.pref.kumamoto.jp/site/arinomama/kokuzoujinja.html Kokuzō Shrine (Kumamoto Prefecture site)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こくそうしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Higo Province]] [[Category:熊本県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥後国の式内社]] [[Category:阿蘇神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の歴史]] [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11421390}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Kokuzō Shrine |画像 = [[File:Kokuzo jinjya.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681|1=Aso, Kumamoto}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya-machi|Q5986245|WD=Q5986245|1=Ichinomiya, Kumamoto}}Teno 2100 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto
Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto
Takahashi-no-Kami
Hinomiya-no-Kami |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |創建 = traditional {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844|1=AD 88}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Kita-no-Miya |例祭 = {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|WD=Q2723|1=July 26}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kumamoto|label=Kokuzō Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kokuzō Shrine''' (こくぞうじんじゃ / くにのみやつこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Teno, Ichinomiya-machi, {{ill|lt=Aso|Q733681|WD=Q733681|1=Aso, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Prefecture|Q130308|WD=Q130308|1=Kumamoto Prefecture}}. Because it stands north of former kanpei-taisha {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639|1=Aso Shrine}}, it is also called '''Kita-no-Miya'''. It is an {{ill|lt=Engishiki shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun}} (ed.), ''Kumamoto Prefecture Encyclopedia'', 1982, p.375. === History and origin === The {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} lists four shrines in {{ill|lt=Higo Province|Q259705|WD=Q259705|1=Higo Province}} (Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto Shrine = present {{ill|lt=Aso Shrine|Q715639|WD=Q715639|1=Aso Shrine}}, Asohime Shrine = present Aso Shrine, Kokuzō Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Hikino Shrine|Q17224057|WD=Q17224057|1=Hikino Shrine}}). Kokuzō Shrine is one of these four and is one of the oldest shrines in Kumamoto Prefecture. According to ''Higo no Kuni-shi'', in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}, Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto was appointed Higo no Kuni no Miyatsuko, and in {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} 18 ({{ill|lt=88 CE|Q24844|WD=Q24844|1=AD 88}}), Kokuzō Shrine was repaired and rituals were organized''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 44: Place Names of Kumamoto Prefecture'', Heibonsha, 1985, p.310. With his father, Aso Shrine's principal deity {{ill|lt=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto|Q10888904|WD=Q10888904|1=Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto}}, he is said to have developed the Aso area and taught agriculture and afforestation. The principal deity is '''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}''' (according to ''{{ill|lt=Kokuzō Hongi|Q133833194|WD=Q133833194|1=Kokuzo Hongi}}'', the first '''{{ill|lt=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q56347021|WD=Q56347021|1=Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko}}'''), and together with his consort and sons, four deities are enshrinedEditorial Committee of Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary (ed.), ''Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.481. The current shrine buildings were built in {{ill|lt=Kanbun|Q1146228|WD=Q1146228|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1672|Q7614|WD=Q7614|1=1672}}) by {{ill|lt=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi|Q11605971|WD=Q11605971|1=Hosokawa Tsunatoshi}}, third lord of the {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Domain|Q1194248|WD=Q1194248|lt=Higo Kumamoto Domain|1=Kumamoto Domain}}Kadokawa Place Names Dictionary 43 Kumamoto Prefecture, Kadokawa Shoten, 1987, p.1297. It became a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in Meiji 5. About 60 m west of the torii are the '''Kamimikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of the enshrined deity Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto, and the '''Shimomikura Kofun''', regarded as the tomb of his consort Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto. These are late-6th-century {{ill|lt=round kofun|Q11394747|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} with {{ill|lt=horizontal stone chambers|Q11543435|WD=Q11543435|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, built of huge {{ill|lt=andesite|Q181908|WD=Q181908|1=Andesite}} blocks and cut Aso lava stone, and are representative megalithic kofun of Kumamoto. === Teno no Sugi === [[ファイル:Tenonosugi.jpg|thumb|right|240px|Preserved remains of Teno no Sugi]] A giant cedar known as "{{ill|lt=Teno no Sugi|Q17217752|WD=Q17217752|1=Teno No Sugi}}" once stood in the precincts''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', p.116.''Natural Monuments of Japan'', p.151.. It was designated a national natural monument on December 9, 1924, under the then Historic Sites, Places of Scenic Beauty and Natural Monuments Preservation LawMinistry of Home Affairs Notice No. 777, Taishō 13. It broke in Typhoon No. 19 (the "Apple Typhoon") on September 27, 1991, and in 2000 the Council for Cultural Affairs recommended de-designation[https://web.archive.org/web/20060111191246/http://www.bunka.go.jp/1hogo/main.asp%7B0fl=show&id=1000007342&clc=1000011213&cmc=1000007006&cli=1000007317&cmi=1000007325%7B9.html Historic Sites / Scenic Places / Natural Monuments (May 2000)] Agency for Cultural Affairs website, viewed 2013-06-10., and it was officially de-designated on September 6, 2000Ministry of Education Notice No. 150, 2000. {{Main|Teno no Sugi}} === Enshrined deities === * Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto - son of Aso Shrine principal deity Takeiwatatsu-no-Mikoto. Aso Kuni no Miyatsuko Ōkami. Enshrined at the 11th shrine of Aso Shrine. * Amiya-hime-no-Mikoto - consort of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. Also called Kamachi-hime-no-Mikoto; sea deity goddess. Principal deity of {{ill|lt=Kōriura Shrine|Q11643225|WD=Q11643225|1=Konoura Shrine}}. * Takahashi-no-Kami - second son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. * Hinomiya-no-Kami - third son of Hayamikatama-no-Mikoto. === Annual festival day === * July 26 === Cultural properties === === Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property (national designation) === * Aso agricultural rites (designated jointly with Aso Shrine) ** Utaizome, spring festival, onda festival, wind festival, nemuri-nagashi, tanomi festival === Aso City designated tangible cultural property === * Shrine hall and haiden[http://www.city.aso.kumamoto.jp/education/cultural-property/list_of_cultural-property/ Aso City website (list of designated cultural properties)] === Precinct shrines === * Kadomori-sha * Suijin-sha * Namazu-no-miya === Access === * 15 minutes by taxi from {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=JR Kyushu|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Hōhi Main Line|Q870826|WD=Q870826|1=Hōhi Main Line}}{{ill|lt=Miyaji Station|Q6884211|WD=Q6884211|1=Miyaji Station}} * 45 km from {{ill|lt=Kyushu Expressway|Q1060803|WD=Q1060803|1=Kyushu Expressway}}{{ill|lt=Kumamoto Interchange|Q1655087|WD=Q1655087|1=Kumamoto interchange}} === Sources === {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{ill|lt=Suzuki Takashi (historian)|Q11648696|WD=Q11648696|lt=Suzuki Takashi|1=Takashi Suzuki}}, ''Shrines and Temples of Kumamoto'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun|Q11568194|WD=Q11568194|1=Kumamoto Nichinichi Shimbun}}, 1980, pp.62-63 * New Kumamoto City History Editorial Committee, ''New Kumamoto City History, Supplementary Vol.2'', {{ill|lt=Kumamoto|Q199889|WD=Q199889|1=Kumamoto}}, 1996, pp.342-345 * Aso Koreyuki (ed.), ''Aso Shrine'', Gakuseisha, 2007, pp.218-220 * {{ill|lt=Numata Makoto|Q11554605|WD=Q11554605|1=Makoto Numata}} (ed.), ''Natural Monuments of Japan 5: Plants III'', {{ill|lt=Kodansha|Q726081|WD=Q726081|1=Kodansha}}, 1984. ISBN 4-06-180585-1, p.116. * Supervised by Cultural Properties Protection Department, Agency for Cultural Affairs, ''Natural Monument Encyclopedia'', {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Dai-ichi Hoki|1=UNKNOWN}}, 1981, p.151. === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Oguni Ryōjinja|Q109299741|WD=Q109299741|1=Oguni Ryo Shrine}} - enshrines Takahashi Ōkami and Hinomiya Ōkami. === External links === {{commonscat|Kokuzō-jinja|Kokuzō Shrine}} * [http://www.pref.kumamoto.jp/site/arinomama/kokuzoujinja.html Kokuzō Shrine (Kumamoto Prefecture site)] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kokuzō Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Higo Province]] [[Category:熊本県の旧県社]] [[Category:肥後国の式内社]] [[Category:阿蘇神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の歴史]] [[Category:熊本県の神社]] [[Category:阿蘇市の建築物]][[Category:qq]]
